title: landolphia foretiana
intro: Landolphia foretiana, also known as the African yam, is a perennial climbing plant native to the tropical forests of Central and West Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a compound called lanatoxin that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Landolphia foretiana include reducing fever, treating rheumatism and arthritis, and providing relief from pain and inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as decoctions and infusions, which are consumed orally or applied topically to treat various health conditions.
